title: When a Super Soldier walks into the gun store... (via: @LouisianaFirearms)
body: "we leave all of our stuff all over the world anyway..." I already knew that, it's just always crazy to hear.
author: @Super_Nova739

title: When a Super Soldier walks into the gun store... (via: @LouisianaFirearms)
body: "We leave all of our stuff all over the world anyway" is the truest statement anyone has ever said... if it was any other item. I have never been in the military, but i know people who have, and there is one common thing they all have said. If rifle is out, it is either in your hands or strapped to your body. If the Sargents even see it laying on your bed, you better be shining a piece that you took off of it or your getting chewed out and getting your room insepcted then and there on a good day. Worse case scenario, it's into the pit and a mountain of paperwork.
author: @mentallyinsane-x2c

title: When a Super Soldier walks into the gun store... (via: @LouisianaFirearms)
body: The end was the best part of this bit for sure! LOL 😂 we leave our stuff everywhere anyway hahaha

title: Boiling 10,000 Crawfish!!! Epic Louisiana Crawfish Throw Down in Cajun Country!!
body: Spuddy is one in a million. A true heart of gold. He went to see his Dad who had alzheimers everyday with plates of delicious food. He fed his Dad every bite. He was an amazing son. He has a true heart of an angel on earth.
author: @sonyahebert9650

title: Boiling 10,000 Crawfish!!! Epic Louisiana Crawfish Throw Down in Cajun Country!!
body: As a Louisiana native, lots of caucasian folks take extreme pride in Cajun food and culture like this man BUT you will almost never hear them acknowledge the influence of the slaves. This man shows extreme respect for the black history of cajun food. I hope his restaurant is still maintaining some operations because I would really like to go.
author: @korykent5645

title: Boiling 10,000 Crawfish!!! Epic Louisiana Crawfish Throw Down in Cajun Country!!
body: Spuddy is such a character - while he seems like such a gentle soul, when he said "If I find you pressure washing the smoker, I would hurt you bad", I absolutely believe him.

title: Louisiana woman accused of refusing to return $1.2M after bank error
body:
If the bank felt that it was an error, she should have made them go through a series of hoops, speak with several of her representatives, placed on hold for an hour before being disconnected and require them to fill out whatever paperwork according to the guidelines she's set. After review, she should reject the claim and if the bank disagrees with her decision, they can send a written request to have the case reviewed by a supervisor.
Just like they do.

title: Louisiana woman accused of refusing to return $1.2M after bank error
body: I remember a case from about 10 or 15 years ago that was similar to this one. A man got over a million in his account by accident. He did nothing and it sat there for almost a week. Then the bank took the money back. The man filed suit and told the judge that they had a right to get their money back but he should be allowed to keep the interest; which was quite a bit of money. The judge ruled he had a right to the interest and he kept it.

title: Wingstop Tenders + Voodoo Fries Taste Test — Honest Review & First Impressions…
body:
Wingstop is my top no.1 place to eat- I LOVE it SO MUCH 😂😭😭😭

I love the Mango Habenaro, Spicy Korean, and Original hot- those are probably my top 3 out of what I’ve tried so far

Edit: I also love Garlic Parmesan- buttery, garlicky- I absolutely love how buttery it is

I also liked their hot honey flavour however I can’t say I absolutely loved it as I don’t exactly remember the taste 😂

I REALLY want to go to Wingstop at some point and try their lemon pepper flavour, I’m not sure what else I wanna try

They have milkshakes as well I’ve seen on the website- I hope they have it at the Bluewater branch as I really wanna try a milkshake
author: @user-yp4wb8bx1n

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body:
Over the course of 2 months of editing, I realized a lot of the video games, anime, movies, and soundtracks I listened to during that time started to influence my video style, especially in the music selection. Can’t remember the amount of times I fell asleep in my chair while searching for the right track, but it was a lot, haha.

A few of inspirations: Your Lie in April, Xenoblade Chronicles, RL Grime, The Bear, Chef (Jon Favreau), Lofi Girl, and Ocean’s Eleven.

Sometimes I’d finish editing for the night, start playing video games, then immediately rush back to editing because of a boss fight, a cutscene, or a musical theme that inspired me to change up a particular song for a scene.

sounds kinda weird, but man I love video games and anime
author: @alvinzhoufilms

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: love seeing a team of young folks absolutely killing it in a super competitive industry.   Proof here that friends can indeed do business together.
author: @davidmills5286

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: Just have to say—this is incredible filmmaking. I watched this because I love barbecue, but after seeing this, you could do a day in the life of an accountant and I’d probably watch it. Great work.
author: @okcharleys

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: This is what America should be. Good people from all different backgrounds working together and doing something that they love as best as they can.
author: @MarfusMcGarfus

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: In September 2022 hurricane Ian hit my town (Fort Myers, FL) and we evacuated to my aunts house a cpl hrs away. While we were there me n my Dad came across this documentary and we both really loved the story. My dad loves good bbq restaurants and has traveled to a lot of different ones all over our state . The next month October 2022 while our town was rebuilding, i got a new job with a $3000 sign-on bonus. I told my mom for Xmas, as one of my dads gifts, I'm using that bonus money to fly me n my dad to Goldees bbq in March for his birthday. One month later on Nov 22, 2022 my father died unexpectedly. It crushed me. But I still took the trip in March 2023 to honor his memory n celebrate his birthday. I brought one of my best friends who my dad really liked in his place. We got there at 6am and we were number 9 n 10 in line. Best bbq I've ever had!  It's painful to watch this video but it also reminds me of my friend who got to experience waiting in the cold for 5 hours for BBQ with me lol. This year, on the same day from last year, me and that same friend went to one of our local barbecue restaurants we like here and had lunch in his memory and in memory of the trip we took. I love n miss you Dad and I'm sorry you didn't get to experience the #1 BBQ in Texas. Thank you Goldees and Alvin for making this unforgettable documentary ❤️🙏🏻
author: @beau2395

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I just drove 1300 miles from Minnesota to texas specifically to eat at goldees and experience genuine texas bbq. I’ve never had anything nearly this good. These fine people don’t just smoke meat, they make art and they are absolute masters at what they do. I WILL be back again!
author: @Sound_And_Color

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I like how she's comfortable enough to be straight up about wanting her own restaurant. This shows the owners don't have big enough egos to want to supress that desire and have confidence they can fill her role.
author: @christianjames92

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: You can tell the reason they’re so successful isn’t necessarily because of the food, it’s because they love the food they cook. They take an insane amount of pride in it. They love the process and every step it takes to get there. And the most important is they love each other.
author: @bretwomble8357

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I'm from Texas, spent over 23 years in the restaurant business, and that is honestly probably one of the best, truest, behind the scenes look at a restaurant I've ever seen, short of being there. I hope this lets people realize the kind of work and dedication that goes into owning and running not just a bbq place, but a restaurant in general. You live and breath it. If you think of getting into the restaurant biz to just make a buck, to start one and then sell it for a bundle, because it's an easy way to make money.... DON'T!!!! you will fail. It's a way of life, not just a "job" or an investment. Fantastic video. Fantastic BBQ.
author: @bendalton5221

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I don't know what it was but this actually made me emotional thinking about back in the day working in a restaurant. Looking back at it you don't remember the early mornings, feeling exhausted or the anxiety involved with a busy service. You remember the good times joking around, prepping food, listening to music, playing video games, taking a smoke break and having real conversations. I don't know exactly what it is but you captured something awesome in this video and it made me feel something for sure.
author: @TrustYourGutz

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: Why am I getting emotional over a barbecue joint in the middle of nowhere in Texas? This art in motion was absolutely beautiful.
author: @Teedeelee

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: As a BBQ lover, this film was so captivating. The cinematography was awesome. The guys at Goldee's seem like such a chill group, no egos at all. Great to see and hear their story.
author: @carguyeyedoc

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I watched this masterpiece for free?!?! What phenomenal work Alvin did. He captured the heart and soul of Goldee's. Absolutely beautiful.
author: @fmilton20

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: This was probably the best doc i've seen in a while. Let them speak for themselves and the passion just comes through them effortlessly. Great job Alvin
author: @ratmdex

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body:
They all seem so chill and get along.
And they're friendly with other venues and hungry to continuing learning and better themselves.
That's some humble #1
And that burnt end of the brisket as a snack for people in line? GOLD
author: @NestlaysChaulkolateChips

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I love how they're busy but they don't rush or give attitude to the customers. Most places when they're super slammed they get annoyed and rude but these guys always keep a calm composure throughout. Great work to you as well Alvin, you're fucking amazing bro.
author: @Mikakurnia1

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I watched the video where the Brits tried this place.  Each of them even their camera crew - when they tried a bite their eyes widened to this point and no words needed - you knew it was amazing.
author: @heidijohnson7231

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: Man, I'm not going to lie in this review. I really thought that I was watching a live action, well thought out production by a high-end company. The fact that this was just done by an individual is INCREDIBLE, and you have earned my subscription. Please keep up the great work!
author: @tylerw3st

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: It’s really amazing to see how young these guys are. Just a bunch of guys that love BBQ come together and open their own place and it just explodes. Hats off of these guys!
author: @jojak06

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: Not only does the food look heavenly, this week-long prep while hanging out with the gang looks and feels like a religious experience I wish I was part of. Stellar filmmaking dude, absolutely amazing
author: @altrogeruvah

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: "a lot of people think what we do is easy" I promise you brother I don't think what you do is easy, mad respect
author: @Trick0ut

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: The guys sitting on the porch at 7am drinking a few beers waiting 4 hours for BBQ is amazing LMAO
author: @nathanhood2415

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: I’m not an American but to me this looks like the American dream to all of those friends and the story told with how this film was out together was incredible!
author: @EvanInTheBackcountry

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body: Wow so Chuck opened her own bbq spot, Barbs BBQ, and it already got awarded the Bib Gourmand. Love to see it
author: @mk21_

title: Day In The Life of The #1 BBQ In Texas
body:
Just got back from Goldee’s. Watching Alvin’s masterpiece of a documentary made me want to get up at 7AM on a weekend and drive 30 minutes to wait in line for 3 hours just for food…
Worth. The. Wait. What those guys (and girls) are doing over there is insane and the care they take of their product is no joke. I’ve had a lot of brisket but this one blew me away with when I tried a piece of the burnt ends while waiting in line. I will be back!
author: @TheClulessDad
